MKT10009 Marketing and the Consumer ExperienceLecture 11
Chapter 14 from our Textbook: Principles of Marketing,
8th edition
Presented by Dr Gordon Campbell

Learning Objectives for Lecture 11 Part 6
14.6 Discuss the need for and value of a legal compliance program in marketing.

Part 6 – Legal compliance in marketing

Legal compliance in marketing (1 of 14)
A legal compliance program is a system designed to identify, manage and reduce the risk of breaking the law.
Almost every decision made in marketing has some legal significance.
Top management commitment and continuing education are key.
Q: Consider some ‘everyday’ marketing decisions (e.g. hiring, packaging, etc). What are some potential legal ramifications of these decisions?

Legal compliance in marketing (2 of 14)
Putting a compliance program in place
AS3806 and its successor, AS/ISO 19600, guide the implementation of a compliance program in marketing.
AS/ISO 19600 focuses on key aspects of compliance:
commitment
implementation
monitoring and measuring
continual improvement.
Q: Australian and International Standards are not legally binding. Should they be?

Figure 14.6
Compliance management system
Source: AS ISO 19600:2015 Fig 1 © Standards Australia Limited. Copied by Pearson Australia with the permission of Standards Australia under Licence 2003-c005.

Legal compliance in marketing (4 of 14)
Legal education
Legal education programs usually cover four sets of relationships that need to be monitored in a legal compliance program:
relationships with competitors
relationships with suppliers
relationships with other parties
relationship with the industry.
Q: What is the role of the ACCC?

Legal compliance in marketing (5 of 14)
Coverage of a legal compliance program
Marketers without a basic understanding of the legislation covering the following areas are living dangerously and potentially putting themselves and their company at risk:
competition law
contract and consumer law
standards
product liability
marketing communication
sales and after-sales finance
franchising
intellectual property.

Legal compliance in marketing (6 of 14)
Coverage of a legal compliance program (cont.)
Competition law
Competition law prohibits various forms of anti-competitive conduct.
The main source of Australian competition law is Part IV of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 and related case decisions.
Q: What is the role of the ACCC?

Legal compliance in marketing (7 of 14)
Coverage of a legal compliance program (cont.)
Contract and consumer law
Laws governing the sale of goods and services broadly cover:
unconscionability
estoppel
consumer protection
complaint management
tendering, outsourcing and partnering
e-commerce.
Key legislation is Sale of Goods Act and Fair Trading Act (in each state) and Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Commonwealth).

Legal compliance in marketing (8 of 14)
Coverage of a legal compliance program (cont.)
Standards
There is a proliferation of standards at global and national levels, within regions and at local level.
Efforts to standardise legal requirements between countries and trading partners are ongoing.
Product liability
Australia and New Zealand have seen a steady growth in class actions in regard to product liability.

Legal compliance in marketing (9 of 14)
Coverage of a legal compliance program (cont.)
Marketing communication
Marketing communications that are seen as being harmful or unfair to consumers may result in significant consequences.
Consumers frequently complain about unwanted messages and unwanted calls.
These are covered by the Spam Act 2003, Do Not Call Register Act 2006 and ACMA standards.
Q: Have you ever seen a television advertisement that you found to be offensive or in poor taste? What did you do about it? Would you respond differently after taking this course?

Legal compliance in marketing (10 of 14)
Coverage of a legal compliance program (cont.)
Sales and after-sales finance
Employees involved in credit negotiations in Australia need to know many aspects of the law, including:
contract law
bills of sale
privacy legislation.
Corporations are covered by the Competition and Consumer Act 2010.
Q: What other aspects of the law do employees involved in these types of negotiations need to know? Do you?

Legal compliance in marketing (11 of 14)
Coverage of a legal compliance program (cont.)
Franchising
Franchising requires specialised legal knowledge due to the exclusive rights and obligations granted under a franchise agreement.
The Franchising Code of Conduct is a mandatory code under the Competition and Consumer Act 2010.
Many other laws apply to franchising.
Q: Many of us can give example(s) of a franchised business. Can you explain what franchising is?

Legal compliance in marketing (12 of 14)
Coverage of a legal compliance program (cont.)
Intellectual property
Intellectual property (IP) law involves such areas as copyright, trademarks, patents, designs, trade secrets and domain names.
Copyright protects ideas as expressed in permanent forms such as original literary, dramatic, musical or artistic works, sound recordings, films and broadcasts.
Design is the overall appearance of a product, resulting from one or more of its visual features.

Legal compliance in marketing (13 of 14)
Intellectual property (cont.)
Trademarks are signs capable of being represented graphically, distinguishing goods or services.
Patents are means of legal protection given to the invention of a device, substance, method or process that is considered useful.
Some IP can be protected only if it treated as confidential information (e.g. trade secrets).
The Competition and Consumer Act 2010 often comes into play in IP marketing cases.

Legal compliance in marketing (14 of 14)
Legal compliance is not a one-off affair
It must be constantly reviewed, audited and updated to keep abreast of important changes in the legal and ethical environment of business.
Q: Surely legal compliance is ultimately the responsibility of upper management? Or is it?

Learning Objective 1 – check that you can >>>
14.6 Discuss the need for and value of a legal compliance program in marketing.

Thank you for your attention
The End of Lecture 11
